review_title: Skype for Kindle, a total failure! Thanks to Amazon Fire OS...
text_review: I've been using Skype for years and It's been a good experience, but not with my new Kindle Fire.I'm not sure what thebig Idea is with amazon and propietrary versions of lots of apps. They all have problems, crashing all the time and making you sorry you bought a kindle and not a Nexus or something different form another manufacturer that can download from the official Google Play Store.Skype is one of those failures, when a nice app you love just drives you crazy for the sake of Amazon's own version of Android. I'm not sure they're aware the ammount of trouble they're causing with that. They think this is the way to capture more customers, but they're totally wrong. And since I hate being forced to buy something that doesn't work, I use alternative stores, even if I can't use the Google Play Store.I updated Skype using a version I found in another store and it works perfectly. Stay away from the "official" kindle version, it's crap, and you'll be sorry in the end.

Sometimes Skype connects and then crashes.  Sometimes the video or audio is one way only and then the app crashes.  I have yet to have a successful Skype call.  Skype worked FINE on the older Kindle Fire HD 7", but not the 8.9".  I have reinstalled twice.  PLEASE FIX THIS APP, IT WAS ONE OF THE MAIN REASONS MY MOTHER GOT THIS KINDLE AS A GIFT -- TO SKYPE HER DISTANT CHILDREN.  I am very disappointed.

It is on my list of Kindle purchases and things I have for my kindle, but I have no idea how it got there. I did not buy it.

review_title: Not enough progress to maintain it as a central tool
text_review: I have a FIOS connection and I can have full bars on the signal and there is always a problem. Always. No matter the time of day. Frozen screens, scattered audio, a lack of synching. Add more than one attendee and it gets very unreliable. With a lot of overseas activity, I have had it for a long time but the point that most stays with me is that it hasn't improved at all over the last 6-7 years. More strides should have been taken by now. As a result, I use it only when the other party has it as a preferred option. Otherwise, I use other apps that do the same thing, only better.

review_title: Skype on Kindle no longer works
text_review: Since updating to the latest version of this app (Oct 2017) I can't receive calls on my Kindle Fire HD.  Inbound calls don't ring or appear on screen.  I can call out to another device AS LONG AS IT ISN'T ANOTHER KINDLE because those calls don't ring on my contacts' Kindles, either. After the fact the app on the Kindle shows a "missed call" as if it had rung and you just didn't answer the call.  Basically, if you are using a kindle, you can now make calls to non-kindle devices but you can not receive a call from any device of any kind.  The text chat feature works, so at least that's something...

First app used on the brand new Kindle Fire HD. Connected without a problem, but the picture quality was very poor, the voice transmission was spotty at best and the call couldn't stay connected. Was it our Wifi? Well, we disconnected the Skype call and dialed up via Facetime and had no problems at all. I'd say it was the Skype.

review_title: and I believe you would have a far better product.
text_review: In my humble opinion, it does not offer enough modern foreign languages that are accessible to native English speakers. While there are a number of approaches to English from other languages, there are few offered to those of us whose mother tongue is English.  I would suggest you add other modern foreign languages, and I believe you would have a far better product.

review_title: Works great on Kindle Fire HD
text_review: If you already have a Rosetta Stone subscription, just sign in and continue your lessons. No issues on the Kindle Fire HD. Works wonderfully.If you do not, then this app is not for you. It will only let you try a sample lesson.So bear that in mind before downloading.But for what it's meant to be, it does well.

Biggest issue I have with it isn't so much that I have trouble learning the languages (I don't), it's that it gives no platform or basis to draw from.  For example, I took three years of HS German and did a study abroad in Germany.  My German isn't good, mostly bar talk and what not.  So yeah, I have a basis to draw from and can "test out" of certain sections, but if I didn't know the difference between I, you, he, she, it, we, you, and they, I'd have trouble with conjugation with no help and I see this when trying the Spanish on here (I took none, and my vocab is limited to Taco Bell and Sesame Street).  It doesn't try to teach you the language so much as add a few words and repetition to what you already have.  Will I continue to use it?  yeah, cause I haven't found anything really good to help as an option.  Will I recommend this to learn a language? No.

review_title: It works with Kindle Fire HD 7" when earbuds and mike are plugged in.
text_review: When I installed this Rosetta Course app, I couldn't get it to work.It would only half load the language I selected and then stop working (with no message to indicate there was a problem).But when earbuds and microphone (3.5mm adapter) are plugged in, the Rosetta Course began to work.The app requires that speakers and a microphone are plugged in and working.I've only just run the first part of the free course they offered, but the Rosetta Course app seems to be very easy to use.Of course, the free course is only going to take you so far.  But it's fine if you just want to know what a Rosetta course is like.

The teaching method needs improvement. For example: they should give the meaning of each word first before they use it in phrases or sentences. Or, they should show the conjugation of verbs first before using them in phrases/sentences. It's easier to remember if there were a list of the words with their corresponding translation.

review_title: Great app to learn another language
text_review: Rosetta Stone has always been the best way to learn another language, and now with this app, it brings the experience to mobile devices. The app was easy to get started with. It allowed me to first start learning the spanish word hola (hi) and then I was prompted to go to complete course mode in the spanish (latin america section). You can of course choose whatever language you would like in the beginning.I didn't like how quickly you have to go with a paid option, but I understand it. I consider that to be the real downside in the app. I love how it associates pictures with the foreign language words. I also liked how I could record my speaking the word into the device, and the device would check my pronunciation  to see how accurate it was. I found some issues sometimes with the app picking up what your saying. You need a good noise free environment, otherwise it will mark what your saying incorrectly.Overall rosetta stone delivers on the promise to teach you the language. It's definitely worth having on your device, especially if your traveling and need to learn the language.

review_title: When you move from one level to the next the ...
text_review: When you move from one level to the next the introduce you to words you have never seen before and expect you to simple know how to translate them.  There ought to be some level of teaching prior to translation ect.  yet there is not even an introduction to the words.

I was about to use this app but read the reviews. I was surprised to learn that it isn't free at all. In fact very expensive if you want the whole course. But they don't say that anywhere in the app description. Just on principle announce I've decided not to encourage this kind of blatant dishonesty.

If all you want to do is learn a few key words, maybe this app is helpful.  But right off the bat, the app was asking me to translate sentences before it even taught me the words to use.  I like that the activities are varied, but there still needs to be a progression to the learning.

review_title: Like it, but room for improvement
text_review: I like this app for my smart TV a lot but there's room for improvement.  I am not sure if all TED talks are available on the app or just some of them. It would be good to have access to all of them. Sometimes the search feature doesn't work as well as I'd like. I tried searching the title, The Puzzle of Motivation, and it would not pull up, but when I searched the speaker, Dan Pink, it did. I tried searching the title with and without "the."  For another TED talk I found on my phone's YouTube app, I couldn't find in the TED app at all. I tried the searching using the title, key words, and the speaker's name. Sometimes when I'm typing in the search bar, it freezes up and goes back to the last screen losing the text I've typed.  I'd also like to have a list of my recently watched videos.

review_title: If I had to pay for it I would be very disappointed. It's just trying to memorize what you've been ...
text_review: It's okay.  If I had to pay for it I would be very disappointed.  It's just trying to memorize what you've been shown.  I don't remember from one time to the next when I try and learn with this program.  I would say that being that it is a free program, it's worth a try.

My daughter has been enjoying the desktop version of this app. I, too, think it is bogus that you should have to pay every year for what you purchase once on a computer. However, my bad review isn't about that. It's about the fact that when you open the app, it offers for you to BUY all the levels of a language without any disclaimer that you are buying an annual subscription as opposed to the software. It is highly unethical wording considering the impulsive and instant method of payment on a phone or kindle. If you had not checked the website separately from the app, you would have no idea.

I hate learning languages this does not change the way I feel but it does make it easier this I'm working on my 5th language... So now I asked the question do I really hate learning languages or do I just hate the process of learning languages regardless of how it's taught.

review_title: I will now purchase Rosetta Stone
text_review: I downloaded this app to 'test drive' Rosetta Stone. I enjoyed it enough th at I will now shell out $500 for the full package.Be aware that the CD or downloaded version of Rosetta Stone comes with a 3 month access to the mobile app; after which, you will have to pay a monthly fee for continued access.

Rosetta is a really good program and it is priced accordingly. The Kindle version works very well , I like it. I have this app as a free trial for one month after purchasing an upgrade to my current version of Spanish for $14..00. No way I will pony up a monthly fee for the convenience of using it on my tablet. It's the same program I've already paid for .  It would be nice to have the option to purchase the Application, but I will not pay for the program content AGAIN. Give us an option for convenience yes, but don't expect me to pay for materials I already own.

I thought this app was amazing when I first got it. I was getting my Spanish on when one day it froze my kindle fire, couldn't even turn off my kindle, totally unresponsive. Once I was able to fix my frozen screen I shrugged it off as a minor glitch on my acutal tablet. But everytime I tried to use Duolingo after that it froze my kindle.
